137 NORTH TENTH STREET

SANTA PAULA, CALIFORNIA 93060 
(80S) 525-7101 
November 15, 1977 
State of California 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Re: 
Conflict of Interest 
Gentlemen: 
I am a private attorney on contract with the 
City of Santa Paula, California, to act as its city attor-
ney. 
Currently, I represent a private client against 
the South Coast Area Transit District, a public entity, 
formed by a joint powers agreement of which the City of 
Santa Paula is a signatory. 
My question to you is whether or not I am pre-
cluded from representing a private individual against the 
South Coast Area Transit District by virtue of the fact 
that it was created by a joint powers agreement executed 
by the City of Santa Paula, an entity which I also repre-
sent. 
Very truly yours, 
PHR: lc

Re: 
Conflict of Interest 
Gentlemen: 
I am a private attorney on contract with the 
City of Santa Paula, California, to act as its city attor-
ney. 
Currently, I represent a private client against 
the South Coast Area Transit District, a public entity, 
formed by a joint powers agreement of which the City of 
Santa Paula is a signatory. 
My question to you is whether or not I am pre-
cluded from representing a private individual against the 
South Coast Area Transit District by virtue of the fact 
that it was created by a joint powers agreement executed 
by the City of Santa Paula, an entity which I also repre-
sent.
sections.conclusion: You are a city attorney for Santa Paula. One of your clients is a party in a manner different from the public generally. All state law prohibits a source of income to you in the government. You have been told that you do not anticipate any involvement in your capacity as City Attorney. Moreover, you stated that you understood you would be prohibited from any such involvement on which you have decided to represent a client in litigation. Last, once the client becomes a source of income to you, you'll be prohibited, for a period of twelve months, from making, particularly in the making or using your official position to influence any future governmental decisions which will directly and materially affect the entity in question in a manner different from the public generally.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a private attorney serving as city attorney for Santa Paula represent a private client against the South Coast Area Transit District, which was formed by a joint powers agreement with Santa Paula?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Once the private client becomes a source of income, the attorney is prohibited for twelve months from using their city attorney position to influence governmental decisions that directly and materially affect that client differently than the general public. The attorney must also avoid any involvement in the city attorney capacity regarding this representation.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains significant OCR errors and formatting issues making some sections difficult to parse. The conclusion section is partially reconstructed from fragmented OCR text. Response letter is dated December 13, 1977 and signed by Ted Prime. Incoming request letter is dated November 15, 1977 from attorney Phillip H. Romney.

Re:

Gentlemen: 
I am a private attorney on contract with the 
City of Santa Paula, California, to act as its city attor-
ney. 
Currently, I represent a private client against 
the South Coast Area Transit District, a public entity, 
formed by a joint powers agreement of which the City of 
Santa Paula is a signatory. 
My question to you is whether or not I am pre-
cluded from representing a private individual against the 
South Coast Area Transit District by virtue of the fact 
that it was created by a joint powers agreement executed 
by the City of Santa Paula, an entity which I also repre-
sent.

CONCLUSION: You are a city attorney for Santa Paula. One of your clients is a party in a manner different from the public generally. All state law prohibits a source of income to you in the government. You have been told that you do not anticipate any involvement in your capacity as City Attorney. Moreover, you stated that you understood you would be prohibited from any such involvement on which you have decided to represent a client in litigation. Last, once the client becomes a source of income to you, you'll be prohibited, for a period of twelve months, from making, particularly in the making or using your official position to influence any future governmental decisions which will directly and materially affect the entity in question in a manner different from the public generally.

embedding.summary: A Santa Paula city attorney sought FPPC advice on whether representing a private client against the South Coast Area Transit District (formed by joint powers agreement with Santa Paula) creates a conflict of interest. FPPC advised that once the client becomes a source of income, a twelve-month prohibition applies on using the city attorney position to influence governmental decisions affecting that client.
